title: Marocaine
blurb: Chef Rachid El Mouhandiz, who scored a 9- in Het Parool, turns his Jordaan spot into a full ode to Moroccan cooking.

title: Daalder* back on the Lindengracht
blurb: Dennis Huwaë moves his Michelin-starred Daalder back to where it started ten years ago — smaller, calmer, sharper.
why: Star chef returns to origin spot

title: De Poldervogel
blurb: Alumni of De Kas and Vuurtoreneiland open a seasonal neighborhood restaurant — refined comfort food, sharing plates, zero fuss.

title: 't Lemmeke
blurb: A country-pub-meets-bistro hybrid landing in West — Dutch cooking with village-inn warmth, opening in a week.

title: Bar Copa
blurb: From the Bar Parry and Balthazar's Keuken people: a tiny weekend-only natural wine bar — wine on tap, Mallorcan charcuterie.

title: Vermut
blurb: A dedicated vermouth-and-aperitivo bar on the JP Heijestraat — Spanish-style early evenings, no reservations culture required.
why: Aperitivo hour finally gets a home

title: Zuiver Wijnen
blurb: Natural wine bar and bottle shop on the IJ-side quay behind Central — low-intervention bottles without the ceremony.

title: De Blauwe Parade
blurb: The 1887 Delft-blue bar is back as a cocktail kroeg under top bartender Milos Stanisic — local spirits, ossenworst, weekly-changing list.

title: Flight Golf
blurb: A golf clubhouse for people who don't own polos — simulators, cocktails and design-led interiors on the NDSM wharf.

title: Île de Bisous
blurb: Oyster bistro Bisous stages a two-day Côte d'Azur fantasy on the water — champagne, small plates and live music.

title: Two Story
venue: Two Story — gallery, design store & café
blurb: An 18th-century canal house rebuilt by Copenhagen's Atelier Axo: design store up front, courtyard café, gallery rotating every three months.
why: Copenhagen design eye, Amsterdam canal house

title: De Parade
blurb: The world's only travelling theatre festival pitches its fairground of tents — 95+ makers of theatre, dance, mime and music plus food stalls and carousel-lit evenings.
why: Travelling theatre fairground · once a year

title: Het Landjuweel
blurb: Amsterdam's oldest festival (since 1975) turns the free-haven artist village of Ruigoord into four days of DJs, live music, rituals and art performances.

title: Milkshake Festival
blurb: Open-minded, gloriously dressed-up dance festival mixing house, disco, pop, techno and dancehall with drag and voguing — kicking off Pride week.

title: WorldPride Amsterdam
blurb: Amsterdam hosts WorldPride 2026: two weeks of citywide street parties and culture, crowned by the world-famous Canal Parade through the grachten.

title: Dekmantel Festival
blurb: Peerless underground electronic festival in the forest — the sharpest techno, house and experimental line-up of the year.
